Text: I just read a poem about the moment you imagine someone you love sitting with your mama in the darkness and warmth. Being fully themselves in your world. And you finally stop dividing yourself because they belong everywhere you belong. That's the moment. [BLOG_LINK]

Text: Nobody mocks you for hiring a personal trainer. Apply the same logic to therapy and suddenly you're 'not strong enough to handle your own life.' We've been carrying an inherited definition of strength that was broken from the start. [BLOG_LINK]

Text: There's a hangover that has nothing to do with alcohol. The kind where someone's laugh replays in your head with cinematic clarity days later — not the important parts, just the way they laughed halfway through a sentence. The mind protects what it believes matters. Even when you'd rather it didn't. [BLOG_LINK]

Text:
There's a version of you most people will never meet. The one who smiled through dinner while something inside broke. The one who said 'I'm fine' so often it became a place you live.

Carrying things nobody sees has a real cognitive cost. It shows up in your work before it shows up in your words.
